title: primula macrophylla subsp. aitchisonii
intro: Primula macrophylla subsp. aitchisonii, commonly known as the Aitchison's primrose or large-flowered primula, is a plant species belonging to the Primulaceae family. It is native to the Himalayas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
